title: Elatostema gracilipes
taxonomy: Elatostema gracilipes is a species of plant in the family Urticaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Elatostema, which consists of about 400 species of stinging nettle-like plants. Elatostema gracilipes is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The classification of Elatostema gracilipes is further divided into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. It is a member of the family Urticaceae and the order Rosales.
uses: Elatostema gracilipes is a species of plant that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. It is reported to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been used to treat conditions such as fever and skin infections. Additionally, it has been used as a treatment for respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Elatostema gracilipes in modern medicine.

intro: Elatostema gracilipes, also known as the Slender Elatostema, is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, contributing to its potential in treating various health conditions such as wounds, infections, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Elatostema gracilipes include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Elatostema gracilipes is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and it is also used in various herbal remedies, such as topical creams and ointments, to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
